context:
<Profile>### IDENTITY
- **Name:** David Park  
- **Age:** 35  
- **Gender:** Male  
- **Experience level:** **Advanced** — 7 years consistent hypertrophy training; 5 NPC men’s physique shows (2022–2024), best placing **2nd at regional**; currently self-coached contest prep.

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):**
  - **NPC Men’s Physique contest prep (muscle-building emphasis while cutting):** arrive stage-ready at **175 lb** (currently **185 lb**) with improved conditioning and proportions.
  - **Bring up priority muscles:** rear delts, lateral delts, **triceps long head**.
- **Secondary goals:**
  - Maintain as much lean mass/strength as possible through a caloric deficit.
  - Keep left knee healthy while training legs hard via joint-friendly patterns.
- **Target events/deadlines:**
  - **Western Regional — July 19, 2025** (noted as **20 weeks out** per intake; **date consistency check:** July 19, 2025 is not 20 weeks from today (2026-02-27). Keeping both as provided—please confirm the actual show date and weeks-out status.)

---

### TRAINING CONTEXT

#### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days:** **5 days/week**
- **Session duration preferences/constraints:** Not provided
- **Time windows:** Not provided
- **Blocked days:** Not provided

#### Equipment & Environment
- **Primary location:** Commercial gym — **LA Fitness**
- **Available equipment (specifics):**
  - Dumbbells **5–120 lb**
  - **8 cable stations**
  - Hammer Strength machines
  - Leg press, hack squat, Smith machine
  - Barbells
  - Full cardio area

#### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE]** Left knee history: **ACL reconstruction (2019)** — avoids **heavy barbell back squats >225 lb** and **deep lunges**; uses **knee sleeves** on leg days; favors **leg press, hack squat, controlled Bulgarian split squats, Smith machine** (since **2026-02-27**, documented). Monitor knee symptoms and loading tolerance.

#### Preferences
- Training style: **controlled tempo**, strong **mind-muscle connection**
- Volume: prefers **high volume (~15–20 sets/muscle/week)**
- Split preference: **PPL–UL hybrid**
- Current nutrition context: **caloric deficit ~2200 kcal/day** (as reported)
- Focus areas: rear delts, lateral delts, triceps long head

---

### METRICS
*(Ordered for bodybuilding priority: body comp first, then strength. No performance numbers were provided yet.)*

**Body Composition**
- **Current body weight:** **185 lb** (as of **2026-02-27**, intake date; user-provided “currently 185”)
- **Target stage weight:** **175 lb** (target date tied to show: **Western Regional, July 19, 2025** — needs date confirmation)
- **Calorie intake:** **~2200 kcal/day** (as of **2026-02-27**)
- **Body fat % / measurements / check-in photos:** Not provided

**Strength (hypertrophy-relevant performance markers)**
- No current lift numbers provided (machines/dumbbells/cables/barbells available).  
  *Recommended to add top-set + rep ranges for key movements (incline press, row, pulldown/pull-up, leg press/hack, RDL, lateral raise variations, rear delt fly/row, overhead extension/skull crusher variants) with dates.*

**Endurance**
- Not provided / not prioritized (cardio equipment available).

**Movement Quality**
- Knee tolerance notes (qualitative): avoids heavy back squats >225 and deep lunges; tolerates leg press/hack/BSS/Smith (as of **2026-02-27**).
- No ROM/pain scale details provided.

---

### LOG
## 2026-02-27 — Intake / Contest-prep profile created
- Added identity details (David Park, 35, male).
- Goals captured: NPC men’s physique prep; stage weight goal **175 lb** from **185 lb**; emphasis on rear delts, lateral delts, triceps long head.
- Training context captured: **5 days/week**, controlled tempo + MMC, high volume **15–20 sets/muscle/week**, **PPL–UL hybrid**, self-coached.
- Equipment captured: LA Fitness (DB 5–120, cables x8, Hammer Strength, leg press/hack/Smith, barbells, cardio).
- Constraint recorded: **left knee ACL reconstruction (2019)**; avoids heavy back squats >225 and deep lunges; uses sleeves; leg press/hack/BSS/Smith tolerated.
- **Data consistency flag:** event date (**July 19, 2025**) conflicts with “**20 weeks out**” given today’s date. Needs confirmation.

--- 

If you confirm (1) the correct show date/weeks-out, (2) your typical session length, and (3) a few current benchmark sets (e.g., leg press, incline DB press, pulldown, lateral raise), I’ll update the METRICS with dated performance markers and tighten the prep timeline in the GOALS/LOG.</Profile>

reference.response:
## Header
**Program name:** Stage-Ready Physique Prep (5-Day PPL–UL Hybrid) — Delt/Triceps Priority + Knee-Friendly Legs  
**Program Owner:** GymText  
**User:** David Park  
**Goal:** NPC Men’s Physique contest prep — cut from ~185 → ~175 while improving conditioning and bringing up rear delts, lateral delts, triceps long head; maintain lean mass/strength; keep left knee healthy  
**Duration:** **Ongoing with phase cycling** (contest peak can be slotted once show date/weeks-out are confirmed)

---

## Program Philosophy
David is an advanced men’s physique competitor cutting in a deficit, so the program prioritizes **hypertrophy stimulus retention** (hard sets close to failure), **fatigue management**, and **joint-friendly exercise selection** to keep training productive while recovery resources are limited. A **5-day PPL–UL hybrid** fits his preference and supports high weekly volume (15–20 sets/muscle/week) while allowing **extra frequency/volume for rear delts, lateral delts, and triceps long head** via targeted “micro-dosing” across the week. Legs are trained hard using **knee-tolerant patterns** (hack/leg press/Smith/BSS with controlled ROM) while avoiding known irritants (heavy back squat loading and deep lunges). Progression is primarily **double progression with RPE targets** (load or reps week-to-week), with a planned deload every 4th week and modest volume reductions as conditioning/cardio demands increase.

---

# Phase 1: Accumulation / Tissue & Volume Base (Weeks 1–4)
**Intent:** Build/maintain muscle with high-quality volume, keep joints happy, establish repeatable performance baselines in a deficit.

### Weekly Pattern

**Day 1 — Push (Chest + Triceps Long Head + Lateral Delt top-up)**  
- **Focus:** Pressing strength maintenance + long-head triceps hypertrophy  
- **Volume:** ~18–24 working sets  
1) Incline DB Press: 3–4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–9  
2) Hammer Strength Chest Press (or Smith Incline): 2–3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
3) Cable Fly (mid-to-low or pec-deck): 2–3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
4) **Overhead Cable Triceps Extension (rope)**: 4 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
   *Long head is biased most when the shoulder is flexed overhead.*  
5) EZ-Bar Skull Crusher **to slight incline** (or DB overhead extension if elbows prefer): 2–3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
6) Cable Lateral Raise: 2–3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9 (2-sec peak)  
   *“Greases the groove” for lateral delts without crushing recovery.*

**Day 2 — Pull (Back + Rear Delts Priority)**  
- **Focus:** Lats/back density + rear delt volume/quality  
- **Volume:** ~20–26 working sets  
1) Neutral-Grip Pulldown (or assisted neutral pull-up): 3–4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–9  
2) Chest-Supported Row (machine or DB): 3–4 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
3) 1-Arm Cable Lat Row (lat bias): 2–3 × 10–14 @ RPE 8–9  
4) **Reverse Pec-Deck (rear delts)**: 4 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9 (1–2 sec squeeze)  
5) Cable Rear-Delt Row (elbows high, wide): 2–3 × 12–16 @ RPE 8–9  
6) Incline DB Curl: 2 × 10–14 @ RPE 8  
7) Rope Hammer Curl: 1–2 × 12–15 @ RPE 8–9

**Day 3 — Legs (Knee-Friendly Quad Emphasis + Hamstrings)**  
- **Focus:** Hard leg stimulus without provoking left knee  
- **Volume:** ~16–22 working sets  
1) Hack Squat (controlled depth you own): 3–4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–9  
   *Quad loading with torso support; depth is self-limited to knee tolerance.*  
2) Leg Press (feet mid, shoulder width): 2–4 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
3) Smith Romanian Deadlift: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
4) Seated Leg Curl: 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
5) Bulgarian Split Squat (controlled, **no deep lunge**): 2 × 8–12/side @ RPE 7–8  
   *Single-leg stability with ROM kept knee-friendly.*  
6) Standing Calf Raise: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
7) Seated Calf Raise: 2 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9

**Day 4 — Upper (Back/Chest Balance + Delt Volume)**  
- **Focus:** Upper body density, extra delts/rear delts  
- **Volume:** ~20–28 working sets  
1) Smith Incline Press (or machine press): 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–9  
2) Seated Cable Row (moderate grip): 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
3) Lat-Focused Pulldown (single or bilateral): 2–3 × 10–14 @ RPE 8–9  
4) Machine Shoulder Press (or DB) — stop 1–2 reps shy of grind: 2–3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7–8  
5) **DB Lateral Raise** (slight forward lean): 4 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
6) **Face Pull (rear delt bias, not traps)**: 2–3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
7) Cable Fly (shortened partials at end): 1–2 × 12–20 @ RPE 9  
8) Optional arms superset (if recovery ok):  
   - Rope Pressdown: 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
   - EZ Curl: 2 × 8–12 @ RPE 8

**Day 5 — “Physique Specialization” (Delts + Triceps + Posing Support)**  
- **Focus:** Priority muscles + pump/metabolic work that doesn’t trash recovery  
- **Volume:** ~18–26 working sets  
1) Cable Lateral Raise (strict): 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
2) Machine Lateral Raise (heavier): 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
3) **Reverse Pec-Deck**: 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
4) Rear-Delt Cable Fly (cross-body): 2 × 15–25 @ RPE 9  
5) **Overhead Cable Triceps Extension**: 4 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
6) Dip Machine (torso upright, triceps): 2–3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
7) Serratus/waist control (for physique lines): Cable Crunch 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 8 + Vacuum practice 3–5 sets of 10–20 sec  
8) **Posing practice:** 10–20 minutes (front/back relaxed, transitions)

---

## Phase 1 Progression Strategy
- **Rep targets & progression (double progression):**  
  - Keep the exercise fixed for the whole phase.  
  - If you hit the **top of the rep range on all sets** at the target RPE, add load next week (small jumps: 2.5–10 lb depending on movement).  
  - If fatigue is high in deficit: add **1 rep per set** before adding load.
- **Effort/RPE:**  
  - Compounds: mostly **RPE 7–9** (avoid frequent true failure).  
  - Isolation: can reach **RPE 9–10** on final set(s) if joints feel good.
- **Deload (Week 4):**  
  - Reduce sets by ~40–50% (keep 1–2 hard sets per movement), maintain load but cap at **RPE 6–7**.
- **Transition trigger to Phase 2:**  
  - After Week 4 deload, or earlier if performance drops for 2 consecutive weeks and soreness/joint stress rises.

---

# Phase 2: Intensification / Strength Retention + Efficient Volume (Weeks 5–8)
**Intent:** Maintain performance on key presses/rows/leg patterns while slightly lowering volume to match increased diet/cardio fatigue. Priority muscles stay high frequency.

### Weekly Pattern (same 5 days; slightly “tighter” volume)

**Day 1 — Push (Heavier top sets + long-head focus)**  
- Incline DB Press: 1 top set × 6–8 @ RPE 9, then 2 back-off × 8–10 @ RPE 8  
- Hammer Strength Press: 2 × 8–10 @ RPE 8  
- Cable Fly: 2 × 12–15 @ RPE 9  
- Overhead Cable Extension: 4 × 8–12 @ RPE 8–9  
- Lateral Raise (cable): 2 × 15–20 @ RPE 9

**Day 2 — Pull (Heavy row + rear delt volume)**  
- Pulldown: 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 8–9  
- Chest-Supported Row: 1 top set × 6–8 @ RPE 9 + 2 × 8–10 @ RPE 8  
- 1-Arm Cable Lat Row: 2 × 10–12 @ RPE 8–9  
- Reverse Pec-Deck: 4 × 12–20 @ RPE 9  
- Cable Rear-Delt Row: 2 × 12–16 @ RPE 9  
- Curls: 3 total sets

**Day 3 — Legs (Knee-friendly, slightly lower total sets)**  
- Hack Squat: 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 8–9  
- Leg Press: 2–3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
- Smith RDL: 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 8–9  
- Leg Curl: 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 9  
- Calves: 4–5 sets total

**Day 4 — Upper (Density + delts)**  
- Smith Incline Press: 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 8  
- Cable Row: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8–9  
- Pulldown: 2 × 10–14 @ RPE 9  
- Machine Shoulder Press: 2 × 8–10 @ RPE 8  
- DB Lateral Raise: 4 × 10–20 @ RPE 9  
- Face Pull / rear delt: 2 × 15–20 @ RPE 9

**Day 5 — Specialization (Delts/Tris pump + posing)**  
- Lateral raise variation A: 3 sets  
- Lateral raise variation B: 3 sets  
- Rear delt: 4–6 sets total  
- Overhead triceps: 4 sets  
- Pressdown/dip machine: 2–3 sets  
- Posing: 15–25 min

### Phase 2 Progression Strategy
- **Top set + back-off model:** aim to keep the top set load stable/increasing even while cutting.  
- **Weekly goal:** add **1 rep** to top set OR add a small load jump while keeping reps.  
- **Volume guardrails:** if sleep/recovery tanks, pull **2–4 sets/week** from non-priority muscles first (chest/back/arms), keep delts and long-head triceps more stable.  
- **Deload:** every 4th week (Week 8) or earlier if knee/elbow irritation emerges.

---

# Phase 3: Pre-Contest “Polish” / High Fatigue Management (Weeks 9–12)
**Intent:** Keep stimulus high but time-efficient; manage systemic fatigue; increase pump/posing emphasis. (This phase also works as a “mini-cut” block if show timing is unclear.)

### Weekly Pattern (5 days; shorter sessions, more supersets)
**Day 1 Push:** maintain incline strength + triceps overhead (reduced total pressing sets)  
**Day 2 Pull:** back maintenance + rear delts high  
**Day 3 Legs:** maintain quads/hams with knee-friendly choices; no grinders  
**Day 4 Upper:** pump/density, minimal joint stress  
**Day 5 Specialization:** delts + triceps + posing priority

**Key implementation rules**
- **Superset a non-competing isolation with compounds** (e.g., incline press ↔ lateral raises; rows ↔ rear delts) to keep intensity without extending sessions.
- **Isolation work can go to near-failure** (RPE 9–10) while compounds stay **RPE 7–8**.

### Phase 3 Progression Strategy
- **Progress by “quality” first:** better execution, longer controlled eccentrics, improved MMC, stable loads at same RPE.  
- **Load progression is optional**; rep progression is preferred.  
- **Deload:** every 4th week, or insert a “recovery micro-week” if weight loss accelerates and performance dips sharply.

---

## Phase Cycling
**Default cycle (non-date-specific):**  
**Phase 1 (Accumulation) → Phase 2 (Intensification) → Phase 3 (Polish) → repeat**, with deloads every 4th week.

**When to move phases**
- **Time-based:** 3 hard weeks + 1 deload week.  
- **State-based:** transition earlier if (a) performance regresses 2 weeks running, (b) joints flare (knee/elbow/shoulder), or (c) sleep/recovery drops and pumps flatten.

**If a confirmed show date exists (recommended once you confirm):**
- Run Phase 1 until ~12–10 weeks out  
- Phase 2 from ~10–5 weeks out  
- Phase 3 from ~5 weeks to peak week  
- Add a **taper** in the final 7–10 days: keep frequency, cut sets by ~30–50%, keep pumps, avoid soreness-inducing novelty.

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial program created from intake profile (5-day PPL–UL hybrid; delts/rear delts/long-head triceps priority; knee-friendly leg selection due to left ACL reconstruction and back squat/deep lunge avoidance).

---

### Notes to tighten this into a true contest-prep plan
If you reply with:  
1) the **actual show date** (and current weeks-out),  
2) your typical **session length**, and  
3) 6–8 benchmark working sets (incline press, row, pulldown, hack/leg press, RDL, lateral raise),  
…I’ll lock in phase lengths to match the show and set more precise load targets/weekly volume landmarks for the cut.
